projects.sickles.title: site.hopen
projects.sickles.title.text
site.hclose
site.br
projects.sickles.overview.title: site.hopen
Overview
site.hclose
projects.sickles.overview.1: Designed and fabricated a series of hand tools from first principles, refining geometry, materials, and construction methods through iterative builds.
projects.sickles.keypoints.title: site.hopen
Key Points
site.hclose
projects.sickles.keypoints.1: Forged blades from flat stock using a custom-built gas forge
projects.sickles.keypoints.2: Developed handle geometry for control and comfort
projects.sickles.keypoints.3: Fabricated ferrules from salvaged steel pipe and washers
projects.sickles.keypoints.4: Iteratively improved edge profile, curvature, and finish
projects.sickles.outcome.title: site.hopen
Outcome
site.hclose
projects.sickles.outcome.1: Produced functional cutting tools with progressive improvements in ergonomics, edge retention, and overall finish. Each build informed the next.
projects.sickles.gallery.title: site.hopen
Gallery
site.hclose
projects.sickles.gallery.embed.1: Gas forge constructed from repurposed gas cylinder.
Provided sufficient and consistent heat for forming and shaping blades.
Steel being heated for sickle 4, in preparaton for initial shaping of tang, and taper, and then subsequent annealing.
projects.sickles.gallery.embed.2: Early blades established basic form but exposed issues with curvature, mass distribution, and edge behaviour.
Further issues were quickly arose as a consequence of ergonomic deficits in the handle.
These limitations were identified through use rather than design alone.
projects.sickles.gallery.embed.3: An iterative improvement in both blade geometry and handle ergonomics resulted in a very serviceable tool that performed as required.
projects.sickles.gallery.embed.4: Improved blade curvature and edge profile following evaluation of earlier builds.
Handle ergnomics regressed during this iteration.
projects.sickles.gallery.embed.5: Surface finishing and polishing introduced to improve cutting performance and corrosion resistance.
projects.sickles.gallery.embed.6: Final blade geometry significantly improved. A square has been included for scale.
Stepwise refinement in three recent blades.
projects.sickles.gallery.embed.7: Blade roughly shaped and annealed ready for profiling.
projects.sickles.gallery.embed.8: Handle, handle with ferrule, and annealed blank for blade.
Handles are pushed onto the tang, whilst it is hot, in order to seat it optimally.
The process is repeated as the blade nears completion.
projects.sickles.gallery.embed.9: Completed sickle with refined handle design and proper blade geometry.
projects.sickles.gallery.embed.10: Ferrules fabricated from galvanised pipe and washers.
Acid bath, Welded, cleaned, and heat treated.
Heat treatment produced oxide finish ("blueing") for corrosion resistance.
projects.sickles.gallery.embed.11: Initial shaping of handle, from green ash, on wood lathe.
Refinement of grip profile based on handling.
Ferrule fit onto handle after turning and polishing.
projects.sickles.discussion.title: site.hopen
Discussion
site.hclose
projects.sickles.discussion.1: Early builds established basic functionality but revealed limitations in blade curvature, edge geometry, and balance. Subsequent iterations focused on refining these aspects through controlled changes to profile and thickness.
projects.sickles.discussion.2: Handle construction evolved alongside blade development. Ferrules were fabricated from readily available materials and heat treated to improve durability. Handle geometry was adjusted to improve grip and control during use.
projects.sickles.discussion.3: The process reflects a practical approach to toolmaking: evaluate performance, identify limitations, and implement targeted improvements in subsequent builds.
projects.sickles.demonstrates.title: site.hopen
What this demonstrates
site.hclose
projects.sickles.demonstrates.1: Iterative design and refinement based on real-world use
projects.sickles.demonstrates.2: Material reuse and practical fabrication techniques
projects.sickles.demonstrates.3: Integration of forging, machining, and finishing processes
projects.sickles.demonstrates.4: Ability to converge on functional, optimised designs
